publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-12-22 18:27:19 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: A collection of underground comic books and related printed material, spanning from approximately 1948-1978. The collection contains precursors to the genre, including 174 Tiajuana Bibles, individual zines, along with galley proofs and proof books of issues of Zap and Motor City. The collection also features an extensive representation of some of the genre's most celebrated titles such as Zap, Bijou Funnies, Motor City, Mr. Natural, Yellow Dog and dozens of others. Most of the issues come from the original publisher's file at The Print Mint, a major publisher of underground comics based in the San Francisco Bay Area during the genre's heyday (1968-1974), and the first publisher to focus heavily on the underground comix movement. The collection includes works by Robert Crumb, Victor Moscoso, Rick Griffin, Joel Beck, Vaughn Bode and many others.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Underground comix are small press, underground or self-published comic books which often present alternative or counter cultural viewpoints. Subjects frequently include alternative lifestyles, satirical challenges to middle-class values and mainstream culture, with regular appearances of recreational drug use, nudity, disrespect for authority, rock n' roll, political dissent, or gratuitous sex and violence. These topics resonated with youth culture of the 1960s and 70s, which set itself at odds with mainstream adult America. Several of the titles present in this collection, such as Horseshit, Zap, Snatch, and the SCUM Manifesto, were banned by various local authorities, resulting in the arrests of some publishers and news dealers for publishing pornography.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: A collection of underground comic books and related printed material, spanning from approximately 1948-1978. The collection contains precursors to the genre, including 174 Tiajuana Bibles, individual zines, along with galley proofs and proof books of issues of Zap and Motor City. The collection also features an extensive representation of some of the genre's most celebrated titles such as Zap, Bijou Funnies, Motor City, Mr. Natural, Yellow Dog and dozens of others. Most of the issues come from the original publisher's file at The Print Mint, a major publisher of underground comics based in the San Francisco Bay Area during the genre's heyday (1968-1974), and the first publisher to focus heavily on the underground comix movement. The collection includes works by Robert Crumb, Victor Moscoso, Rick Griffin, Joel Beck, Vaughn Bode and many others.

emph.ref: Series VI. Series 6. 50 duplicates from the Print Mind publisher's file

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Underground Comix collection, #8644. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Galley Proof with Photostat pages for Zap #4. Print Mint file copy. Plain brown wrappers with "ZAP" lettered in ink on the upper cover. Camera ready pages pasted in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page Layout Dummy. Original drawings on the upper cover (in pencil) and lower cover (in ink). Original ink by Victor Moscoso. Pages largely blank, presumably awaiting pasted in camera ready artwork, with penciled annotations indicating which panels belong on each page.

did.unittitle: Black and white proofs Zap issue #3
Black and white proofs Zap issue #3, #4
Proof sheets for Motor City Comics #2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 38 Proofs in various states of color separation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Front cover art by R. Crumb, contributions by Crumb, S. Clay Wilson, Wes Wilson, Victor Moscoso, and Rick Griffin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One of the early countercultural newspapers to emerge, frequently featuring comic strips by R. Crumb, Kim Deitch, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One of the early countercultural newspapers to emerge, frequently featuring comic strips by R. Crumb, Kim Deitch, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poster which came with Oz Magazine no. 24, published in the UK. On the reverse is a replica of the front page from the UK newspaper The People

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Heritage Auctions sitcker on the back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 st edition of the second underground comix, the first with an anti-war theme. 11 copies known. Vaughn Bode's personal copy with final changes and corrections.

did.unittitle: Foo! Lampoons and Parody Vol. 1 No. 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed first edition by Jack Jackson

did.unittitle: My Own Mag No. 5 and No. 7

scopecontent.p: William Burroughs, Special Tangier Edition and Moving Times

scopecontent.p: "Good, clean all-american ssex, pot party, Bar BQ blast"

scopecontent.p: Green shoes and green pants on front cover

scopecontent.p: Brown shoes and green pants on front cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contributions by Greg Irons, Rick Griffin, Moscoso, et al.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes R. Crumb cartoon on interior of rear cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In 1968, Horseshit was responsible (along with Zap, Snatch, and the SCUM Manifesto) for the arrest of Berkeley bookseller Moe Moskowitz on charges of selling pornography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comic strips and graphic tales by Bill Bergeron

did.unittitle: Complete Set of Topps Bubble-Gum cards
Series 6. 50 duplicates from the Print Mind publisher's file
The Kingdom of Heaven is Within You Comix No.1
